title: pisum commune subsp. arvense
intro: Pisum commune subsp. arvense, also known as garden pea or field pea, is a subspecies of the Pisum commune plant, a member of the legume family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its emollient and expectorant qualities, which have been utilized to soothe irritated skin and relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The health benefits of Pisum commune subsp. arvense include its ability to act as a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Pisum commune subsp. arvense can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
